occupations.title: Sales Assistants (General)
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sales Assistants operate primarily in physical retail environments, requiring face-to-face customer interaction, handling of physical goods, and store maintenance. While AI will impact the broader retail sector through advanced inventory forecasting and the continued shift to e-commerce, the core daily tasks of in-store sales assistants are largely shielded from direct digital AI automation.

occupations.title: Aged and Disabled Carers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Aged and Disabled Carers perform highly physical and interpersonal tasks, providing hands-on care, household assistance, and emotional support. AI cannot replicate the physical presence, empathy, and manual assistance required in this role. While AI may streamline some administrative or scheduling tasks, the core responsibilities remain entirely insulated from digital automation, especially given Australia's growing demand and severe shortages in the care sector.

occupations.title: Registered Nurses
occupations.exposure_rationale: Registered nursing involves significant hands-on patient care, physical presence in clinical or aged care settings, and complex interpersonal communication, which are highly resistant to AI automation. However, AI will increasingly assist with medical record keeping, patient monitoring, and diagnostic support, moderately reshaping the administrative and analytical aspects of the role.

occupations.title: General Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: General clerical work involves routine data processing, document handling, and basic communication, all of which are highly susceptible to automation by current AI technologies. In the Australian corporate and government sectors, AI-driven productivity gains in these administrative tasks will likely significantly reduce the demand for human clerks.

occupations.title: Retail Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Retail Managers require a strong physical presence to oversee store operations, manage staff, and handle customer interactions, which limits direct AI replacement. However, AI will significantly streamline their administrative duties, such as inventory forecasting, staff rostering, and sales analytics. Overall exposure is moderate, as AI acts as a powerful back-office tool rather than a substitute for on-the-floor leadership.

occupations.title: Accountants
occupations.exposure_rationale: Accounting involves significant digital data processing, financial reporting, and compliance work, which are highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven analysis. While AI will streamline routine tax and audit tasks, Australian accountants will still be needed for strategic advisory, complex regulatory interpretation, and client relationship management.

occupations.title: Software and Applications Programmers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Software programming is entirely computer-based and directly targeted by current generative AI capabilities such as code generation, debugging, and documentation. In Australia's tech sector, AI tools will drastically increase individual developer productivity, fundamentally reshaping day-to-day tasks and potentially altering future hiring demand for entry-level coders.

occupations.title: Electricians
occupations.exposure_rationale: Electricians perform highly physical, manual work in unpredictable real-world environments like construction sites, mines, and domestic properties, which current digital AI cannot replicate. While AI may streamline minor administrative tasks like invoicing, scheduling, or looking up schematics, the core tasks of installing, diagnosing, and repairing electrical systems require hands-on expertise. Given the severe labour shortage and physical nature of the trade in Australia, AI exposure is very low.

occupations.title: Truck Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Truck driving is a highly physical occupation requiring real-world navigation and manual operation of heavy machinery. While AI can assist with route optimization and logistics planning, current digital AI capabilities cannot replace the physical task of driving and handling goods. Autonomous vehicle technology is not expected to significantly displace these workers in Australia over the next 5 years.

occupations.title: Child Carers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Childcare is fundamentally a physical and interpersonal occupation requiring high emotional intelligence and hands-on supervision. While AI may assist with administrative tasks like scheduling or parent communication, the core responsibilities of caring for children cannot be automated. This is a major employment category in Australia with significant labour shortages, and AI will not replace the need for human presence.

occupations.title: Receptionists
occupations.exposure_rationale: While the physical presence of welcoming visitors provides some barrier to full automation, a significant portion of a receptionist's role involves scheduling, answering inquiries, and routing communications. AI voice agents, chatbots, and automated booking systems are rapidly absorbing these routine administrative tasks, leading to high exposure and likely reducing the number of receptionists needed in Australian offices and clinics.

occupations.title: Advertising, Public Relations and Sales Manager
occupations.exposure_rationale: Advertising, PR, and Sales Managers rely heavily on data analysis, content generation, and market research, all of which are highly exposed to current generative AI capabilities. However, their core role involves strategic decision-making, relationship building, and team leadership, which provides a buffer against full automation. In the Australian corporate landscape, AI will significantly augment their productivity, reducing the need for junior support staff while reshaping the manager's day-to-day tasks.

occupations.title: Storepersons
occupations.exposure_rationale: Storepersons perform highly physical tasks involving the receipt, handling, and dispatch of goods in warehouses. While AI will improve inventory management software and supply chain logistics, the core physical labor cannot be automated by current digital AI capabilities. Therefore, exposure to digital AI is low, though future physical robotics may eventually impact the role.

occupations.title: Primary School Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, the core role of a primary school teacher relies heavily on interpersonal skills, emotional intelligence, and physical classroom management. In the Australian context, the strong focus on the social, emotional, and physical development of young children creates a natural barrier to high AI exposure.

occupations.title: Secondary School Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Secondary school teaching involves a mix of knowledge work, such as lesson planning and grading, and intensive interpersonal work, like classroom management and emotional support. While AI will significantly streamline administrative and curriculum preparation tasks, the core requirement for in-person student engagement and social development in the Australian education system limits overall exposure.

occupations.title: Contract, Program and Project Administrators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Contract and project administration involves heavy document processing, schedule tracking, and report generation—tasks highly susceptible to generative AI and automated project management tools. While stakeholder management requires human oversight, AI will significantly boost productivity, meaning fewer administrators will be needed to manage the same number of projects in Australia's corporate and government sectors.

occupations.title: Commercial Cleaners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Commercial cleaning is an entirely physical occupation requiring manual dexterity and mobility in varied, unpredictable environments. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate or significantly augment the core tasks of cleaning offices, hospitals, or industrial sites. Therefore, AI exposure is minimal, limited only to potential backend scheduling or administrative efficiencies.

occupations.title: Carpenters and Joiners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity, spatial problem-solving, and adaptability to unpredictable construction environments. While AI may assist independent tradies with minor administrative tasks like quoting or scheduling, the core work of cutting, shaping, and installing materials cannot be automated by current digital AI. Consequently, this occupation is highly insulated from AI disruption in the Australian construction sector.

occupations.title: Kitchenhands
occupations.exposure_rationale: Kitchenhands perform highly physical, manual tasks such as cleaning, washing dishes, and basic food preparation in commercial kitchens. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate these physical, real-world actions. While AI might optimize restaurant inventory or scheduling, the core day-to-day labor of a kitchenhand remains entirely insulated from digital AI disruption.

occupations.title: Office Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Office Managers handle administrative systems, scheduling, and personnel coordination, much of which is highly exposed to AI automation and augmentation. While AI can streamline reporting, resource allocation, and communications, the role still requires interpersonal skills for managing staff and overseeing physical office environments. In the Australian context, this will likely lead to significant productivity gains, allowing one manager to oversee larger or multiple offices.

occupations.title: Accounting Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Accounting clerks perform highly routine, computer-based tasks such as processing invoices, calculating costs, and managing accounts. These digital, rules-based processes are prime targets for automation and current AI capabilities, which can easily extract data from documents and reconcile accounts. Consequently, this occupation faces very high exposure to AI-driven displacement in the Australian market.

occupations.title: Education Aides
occupations.exposure_rationale: Education Aides require physical presence, emotional intelligence, and direct interpersonal interaction to supervise and assist students in Australian classrooms. While AI may streamline some minor administrative or resource-preparation tasks, the core responsibilities of student care, behavioral support, and hands-on assistance cannot be automated.

occupations.title: Construction Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Construction managers in Australia handle a mix of digital planning, such as scheduling and budgeting, alongside highly physical and unpredictable on-site coordination. While AI will significantly streamline project management, cost estimation, and compliance tracking, the core role requires physical presence, stakeholder negotiation, and real-time problem-solving on active building sites, limiting overall exposure.

occupations.title: Chefs
occupations.exposure_rationale: Chefs perform highly physical and sensory tasks in fast-paced kitchen environments, which digital AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with administrative duties like menu planning, inventory management, and cost analysis, the core preparation and cooking of food requires manual dexterity and physical presence.

occupations.title: Waiters
occupations.exposure_rationale: Waiters perform highly physical and interpersonal tasks in unpredictable environments like restaurants and cafes, which digital AI cannot replicate. While AI-driven ordering systems may streamline order-taking, the core duties of physically serving food, clearing tables, and providing human hospitality remain entirely insulated from digital automation.

occupations.title: Purchasing and Supply Logistics Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Purchasing and supply logistics clerks primarily perform routine digital tasks such as processing orders, monitoring inventory, and scheduling. These data-heavy, computer-based processes are highly susceptible to automation through AI-driven predictive analytics and automated supply chain management systems. In Australia's logistics and retail sectors, the push for efficiency will likely see AI significantly reduce the need for manual clerical intervention in these roles.

occupations.title: Metal Fitters and Machinists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Metal fitting and machining is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity, spatial awareness, and on-site presence, particularly in Australia's large mining and manufacturing sectors. While AI may assist in optimizing CNC programming or predictive maintenance schedules, the core tasks of assembling parts and operating machinery cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Checkout Operators and Office Cashiers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While checkout operators require physical presence in retail environments, their core transactional tasks are highly routine and increasingly automated. They face significant exposure from AI-driven computer vision systems in self-checkout kiosks and automated payment processing software, though the physical handling of goods prevents a maximum score.

occupations.title: Bar Attendants and Baristas
occupations.exposure_rationale: Bar attendants and baristas perform highly physical and interpersonal tasks that require real-world presence and manual dexterity. While AI may streamline inventory management or point-of-sale systems, the core duties of preparing beverages and interacting with customers remain largely insulated from digital AI automation.

occupations.title: Motor Mechanics
occupations.exposure_rationale: Motor mechanics perform highly physical and manual tasks that require direct interaction with vehicles and engines, which current digital AI cannot automate. While AI may assist with vehicle diagnostics and inventory management, the core repair and maintenance work remains entirely physical. In the Australian context, trades like this are shielded from significant AI disruption due to the necessity of hands-on mechanical skills.

occupations.title: Nursing Support and Personal Care Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Nursing support and personal care work is fundamentally physical and interpersonal, requiring hands-on assistance with daily living and patient care. In the Australian context, particularly with the aging population and NDIS, demand for human-delivered care remains robust. AI will only marginally impact this role by streamlining administrative tasks like rostering or basic care documentation, leaving the core physical duties unaffected.

occupations.title: Plumbers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Plumbers perform highly physical tasks in unpredictable, real-world environments that current AI and robotics cannot navigate or manipulate. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling, quoting, or invoicing, the core work of installing and repairing pipes and drainage systems remains entirely manual. In the Australian context, trades like plumbing are in high demand and face minimal disruption from digital AI.

occupations.title: Solicitors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Solicitors rely heavily on text analysis, legal research, and document drafting, which are highly susceptible to automation and augmentation by current generative AI. However, the requirement for complex human negotiation, strategic counsel, and courtroom representation prevents full automation, placing their exposure in the high category.

occupations.title: Management and Organisation Analysts
occupations.exposure_rationale: Management and Organisation Analysts primarily perform knowledge work, including data analysis, process mapping, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. However, the role also requires significant stakeholder engagement, change management, and complex problem-solving in unpredictable human environments, which provides some buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Real Estate Sales Agents
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the administrative and marketing tasks of real estate agents, such as drafting property descriptions, managing listings, and handling initial client inquiries. However, the core of the role relies heavily on interpersonal trust, physical property inspections, and high-stakes negotiations in the competitive Australian property market, providing a strong barrier to full automation.

occupations.title: Advertising and Marketing Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Advertising and marketing rely heavily on content creation, data analysis, and digital strategy, all of which are core strengths of current generative AI. While human oversight and strategic direction remain important, AI tools can automate significant portions of copywriting, market research, and campaign optimization, leading to high exposure.

occupations.title: General Practitioners and Resident Medical Officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: General Practitioners perform a mix of complex knowledge work, physical examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication. While AI will significantly reshape administrative tasks like clinical note-taking and assist in diagnostics, the need for physical presence, empathy, and legal accountability provides a strong barrier to automation. In the Australian context, where there is a critical shortage of GPs, AI will likely act as a productivity enhancer rather than replacing workers.

occupations.title: ICT Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: ICT Managers perform highly computer-based knowledge work, making them significantly exposed to AI tools that streamline project management, system design, and data analysis. However, their core responsibilities involve strategic decision-making, stakeholder management, and human coordination, which require interpersonal skills and judgment that AI cannot fully replace.

occupations.title: Human Resource Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: HR Managers handle a mix of strategic planning, complex workplace relations, and administrative tasks. While AI will significantly streamline policy drafting, recruitment screening, and HR analytics, the core responsibilities of managing employee relations, navigating Australia's Fair Work legislation, and resolving conflicts require high emotional intelligence and human judgment.

occupations.title: Welfare Support Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Welfare Support Workers rely heavily on interpersonal skills, empathy, and human connection to assist vulnerable clients, which AI cannot replicate. While AI tools will likely streamline case management, paperwork, and resource coordination, the core face-to-face support remains fundamentally human.

occupations.title: Bookkeepers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Bookkeeping consists almost entirely of routine digital processing, data entry, and financial reconciliation, which are prime targets for AI automation. In Australia, the widespread adoption of cloud accounting platforms like Xero and MYOB is already integrating AI to automate bank feeds and invoice processing. This will drastically reduce the hours needed per client, leading to very high AI exposure and a likely reduction in overall workforce demand.

occupations.title: Other Hospitality, Retail and Service Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: These management roles require significant physical presence to oversee facilities, equipment, staff, and customer interactions in real-world settings like cinemas, kennels, and retail branches. While AI will streamline administrative tasks such as rostering, inventory management, and financial reporting, the core responsibilities of managing physical operations and handling unpredictable interpersonal situations provide a strong barrier to automation.

occupations.title: Information Officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Information Officers primarily handle routine inquiries, complaints, and information provision via text and voice. These tasks are highly exposed to current generative AI capabilities, such as advanced conversational agents and automated ticketing systems. While some face-to-face roles exist, the bulk of this administrative work can be heavily automated or augmented, leading to significant reshaping of the occupation.

occupations.title: Gardeners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Gardeners perform highly physical, outdoor work that requires manual dexterity and adaptation to unpredictable environmental conditions, which AI cannot automate. While AI applications may assist with landscape design or plant disease identification, the core duties of planting and maintaining gardens require physical presence, resulting in minimal AI exposure.

occupations.title: Delivery Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Delivery driving is an inherently physical occupation requiring navigation of unpredictable real-world environments and manual handling of goods. While AI will improve route optimization and logistics software, current digital AI cannot replace the physical act of driving and delivering packages, keeping exposure minimal over the next five years.

occupations.title: Human Resource Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: HR professionals perform predominantly knowledge-based work, such as drafting policies, screening candidates, and analyzing workforce data, which are highly exposed to AI automation. However, complex tasks like dispute resolution, enterprise bargaining, and managing sensitive interpersonal issues require human empathy and judgment, preventing full automation.

occupations.title: Architectural, Building and Surveying Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly streamline estimating, drafting plans, and evaluating survey data, a substantial portion of this role involves physical site inspections and supervision. This mix of on-site physical presence and computer-based technical work results in moderate AI exposure.

occupations.title: Structural Steel and Welding Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Structural steel and welding work is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, spatial awareness, and operation in unpredictable real-world environments like construction sites and shipyards. Current digital AI capabilities cannot perform these hands-on fabrication and repair tasks. While AI might eventually assist with blueprint interpretation or minor administrative duties, the core trade remains entirely insulated from digital AI disruption.

occupations.title: Finance Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Finance Managers primarily engage in computer-based knowledge work, including financial analysis, reporting, and forecasting, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, their roles also involve strategic decision-making, leadership, and stakeholder management, providing a buffer against full automation. In the Australian corporate sector, AI will significantly enhance their productivity, potentially reducing the need for larger finance teams.

occupations.title: ICT Support Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI and advanced language models are highly capable of automating Level 1 IT support, diagnosing software issues, and resolving common technical problems via intelligent ticketing systems. However, the role retains a physical component involving the deployment and maintenance of hardware infrastructure, which provides a buffer against total automation. Overall, the occupation will see significant reshaping as routine troubleshooting shifts to AI, requiring technicians to focus on complex or physical infrastructure tasks.

occupations.title: Civil Engineering Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI will significantly streamline structural analysis, CAD design, and cost estimation tasks for civil engineers. However, the role still requires physical site oversight, complex stakeholder management, and strict regulatory compliance within Australia's infrastructure sector, preventing full automation.

occupations.title: Other Miscellaneous Labourers
occupations.exposure_rationale: These roles are almost entirely physical and require on-site presence, such as directing traffic, collecting trolleys, or assisting tradespeople. Digital AI has virtually no direct impact on the core manual tasks performed by these workers. Any AI integration would be limited to backend scheduling or logistics rather than the jobs themselves.

occupations.title: Database and Systems Administrators, and ICT Security Specialists
occupations.exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely computer-based, making it highly exposed to AI tools that can automate scripting, log analysis, threat detection, and database optimization. While AI will significantly boost productivity and reshape daily tasks, the need for human accountability, strategic planning, and complex system architecture design in Australian enterprise and government sectors prevents full automation.

occupations.title: Livestock Farmers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Livestock farming is inherently physical and tied to unpredictable real-world environments, requiring hands-on management of animals and farm infrastructure. While AI will increasingly assist with predictive analytics, weather forecasting, and farm management software, the core operations cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Other Specialist Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Specialist managers perform a significant amount of knowledge work, including reporting, compliance, and data analysis, which are highly exposed to AI automation. However, their roles also require complex stakeholder management, leadership, and domain-specific oversight that cannot be fully automated. In the Australian context, AI will significantly enhance their productivity in administrative tasks while leaving the core interpersonal and strategic responsibilities intact.

occupations.title: Drillers, Miners and Shot Firers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Drillers, miners, and shot firers operate in highly physical, unpredictable, and hazardous environments that are central to Australia's massive resources sector. While AI may assist mining engineers with geological analysis or blast planning, the core tasks of assembling rigs, operating heavy machinery, and handling explosives require physical presence and manual execution, making direct exposure to digital AI minimal.

occupations.title: Early Childhood (Pre-primary School) Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Early childhood teaching is highly interpersonal and requires physical presence to manage, nurture, and educate young children. While AI can assist with lesson planning and administrative reporting, the core tasks of emotional support, behavioural management, and hands-on learning cannot be automated. The ongoing labour shortage in Australia further highlights the strong, irreplaceable demand for human workers in this sector.

occupations.title: Sales Representatives
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sales Representatives spend a significant amount of time on lead generation, CRM management, and email communication, all of which are highly exposed to AI automation. However, building trust, negotiating B2B deals, and maintaining client relationships still require a strong human element, preventing complete automation but significantly increasing productivity.

occupations.title: Shelf Fillers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Shelf filling is an entirely physical occupation requiring manual dexterity and physical presence in retail environments. While AI may optimize backend inventory management and ordering systems, current digital AI cannot automate the physical task of stocking shelves, keeping exposure minimal.

occupations.title: Forklift Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Forklift driving is a highly physical occupation requiring manual operation of machinery in dynamic environments like warehouses and yards. While AI may optimize warehouse logistics and inventory management, current digital AI capabilities cannot replace the physical task of operating the vehicle, keeping exposure minimal.

occupations.title: Police
occupations.exposure_rationale: Police work requires significant physical presence, community engagement, and real-time judgment in unpredictable environments, providing a strong barrier to AI replacement. However, AI will increasingly automate the heavy administrative burden of report writing and assist in evidence analysis and predictive policing. This results in a moderate exposure where AI acts as an investigative and administrative tool rather than a replacement.

occupations.title: Building and Plumbing Labourers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Building and plumbing labourers perform highly physical, manual tasks in unpredictable construction environments. Digital AI cannot automate the physical labour required for excavating, clearing sites, or installing pipes, keeping exposure minimal.

occupations.title: General Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: General Managers rely heavily on complex decision-making, strategic planning, and interpersonal leadership, which act as strong barriers to full AI automation. However, AI will significantly reshape their roles by automating data analysis, report generation, and operational monitoring, enhancing their strategic capabilities. While AI will streamline the analytical aspects of management in Australian corporate and government sectors, the core requirements of human accountability and stakeholder negotiation keep their exposure from being very high.

occupations.title: Security Officers and Guards
occupations.exposure_rationale: Security officers require physical presence to patrol premises, manage crowds, and respond to incidents in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI-enhanced surveillance systems and computer vision will automate some monitoring tasks, the core physical and interpersonal aspects of the role remain highly shielded from AI replacement.

occupations.title: Sports Coaches, Instructors and Officials
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sports coaching and officiating require physical presence, real-time decision making, and strong interpersonal skills to motivate and instruct athletes. While AI can assist with performance data analysis and scheduling, the core responsibilities are inherently physical and human-centric, limiting direct AI automation.

occupations.title: Hairdressers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Hairdressing is an inherently physical and interpersonal occupation requiring manual dexterity and real-world interaction with clients. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like appointment scheduling or virtual hairstyle previews, the core tasks of cutting, colouring, and styling hair cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: University Lecturers and Tutors
occupations.exposure_rationale: University teaching and research involve extensive knowledge work, including curriculum design, grading, and literature reviews, which are highly exposed to generative AI. While in-person mentoring and complex research provide a buffer, AI's ability to automate marking and act as a personalized tutor may significantly reduce the reliance on casual academic staff in Australia's large higher education sector.

occupations.title: Production Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production Managers in Australia's key industries like mining, forestry, and manufacturing involve a mix of complex logistical planning and on-site human management. While AI will significantly enhance supply chain optimization, scheduling, and predictive maintenance, the role's reliance on overseeing physical operations and managing personnel provides a strong buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Cafe and Restaurant Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Cafe and Restaurant Managers operate in highly physical and interpersonal environments, requiring on-site presence to manage staff, oversee customer service, and handle unpredictable daily operations. While AI will streamline administrative tasks like rostering, inventory forecasting, and marketing, the core responsibilities of hospitality management remain shielded from direct AI automation.

occupations.title: Financial Investment Advisers and Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Financial advising and investment management rely heavily on data analysis, market research, and report generation, which are highly exposed to AI automation. However, the Australian financial services sector is strictly regulated, and the role requires significant interpersonal skills to build client trust and navigate complex personal circumstances, preventing complete automation.

occupations.title: Chief Executives and Managing Directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Chief Executives and Managing Directors perform highly strategic knowledge work that will be significantly augmented by AI through advanced data analysis, market forecasting, and streamlined communications. However, the core responsibilities of human leadership, complex stakeholder negotiation, and ultimate legal and corporate accountability provide a strong barrier to direct automation.

occupations.title: Graphic and Web Designers, and Illustrators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Graphic and web design work is almost entirely digital and computer-based, making it highly susceptible to disruption by generative AI. Tools that automate image generation, layout design, and web coding are rapidly advancing, which will drastically increase productivity and potentially reduce the total number of designers needed in the Australian market.

occupations.title: Supply, Distribution and Procurement Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Supply and procurement management involves significant data analysis, demand forecasting, and contract management, all of which are highly exposed to AI optimization. However, the role also requires strategic negotiation, relationship management with suppliers, and handling real-world logistical disruptions, which provides a buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Other Information and Organisation Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: These roles involve heavy information processing, document analysis, and written communication, which are highly exposed to generative AI and automation. While interpersonal liaison and complex regulatory judgment provide some buffer, AI will significantly reshape how these professionals handle research, drafting, and administrative tasks in the Australian context.

occupations.title: Packers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Packers perform highly physical, manual tasks involving the handling and packaging of real-world goods such as agricultural produce and meat. While warehouse automation and physical robotics may impact this role, current digital AI capabilities like language processing and data analysis have almost no direct application to these physical tasks.

occupations.title: Psychologists and Psychotherapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI tools can significantly streamline clinical note-taking, report generation, and initial patient intake, the core of psychology and psychotherapy relies on deep interpersonal connection, empathy, and nuanced behavioral observation. In the Australian healthcare context, strict regulatory standards and the necessity of human trust in mental health treatment provide a strong barrier to full automation, meaning AI will act primarily as an administrative co-pilot.

occupations.title: Financial Brokers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Financial brokering involves significant data processing, risk assessment, and document generation, all of which are highly exposed to AI automation. While relationship building and complex negotiations provide some buffer, AI tools will drastically increase productivity in loan and insurance matching, likely reshaping the profession.

occupations.title: Payroll Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Payroll clerks perform highly routine, computer-based tasks involving data processing, calculations, and statutory record-keeping. In the Australian context, AI systems are increasingly capable of automatically interpreting complex Fair Work awards and processing payroll with minimal human intervention, making this role highly susceptible to direct automation.

occupations.title: Earthmoving Plant Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Earthmoving plant operators work in highly physical and unpredictable environments, primarily in construction and mining. While some large-scale Australian mining operations use automated machinery, this relies on specialized robotics rather than current digital AI capabilities. Consequently, direct exposure to generative AI is minimal.

occupations.title: Health and Welfare Services Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Health and Welfare Services Managers handle significant administrative, planning, and coordination tasks that AI can heavily streamline, such as resource allocation, reporting, and compliance monitoring. However, their roles require complex human judgment, leadership, and stakeholder management within Australia's highly regulated and sensitive healthcare and social assistance sectors, meaning AI will act primarily as a decision-support tool rather than a replacement.

occupations.title: ICT Business and Systems Analysts
occupations.exposure_rationale: ICT Business and Systems Analysts perform entirely computer-based knowledge work, making them highly exposed to AI. Generative AI excels at drafting system documentation, mapping business processes, and translating user requirements into technical specifications. While stakeholder management and complex business alignment provide some buffer against full automation, AI will significantly boost individual productivity, likely reducing the total number of analysts required in the Australian tech sector.

occupations.title: Personal Assistants
occupations.exposure_rationale: Personal assistants primarily handle scheduling, correspondence, and coordination tasks that are highly digital and increasingly automated by AI tools like smart calendars and language models. While some interpersonal liaison remains, the bulk of the administrative workload can be significantly streamlined, reducing the overall need for dedicated assistants.

occupations.title: Private Tutors and Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Teaching subjects like art, dance, drama, and music relies heavily on physical presence, real-time interpersonal feedback, and nuanced human expression. While AI can assist with lesson planning or generating theory exercises, the core performance and practical coaching aspects remain highly resistant to automation.

occupations.title: Automobile Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Automobile driving is an entirely physical occupation requiring real-world navigation and vehicle operation, insulating it from current digital AI capabilities. While autonomous vehicle technology is advancing, widespread replacement of drivers in Australia within the next five years remains unlikely due to regulatory and technical hurdles. AI's current impact is limited to route optimization and ride-hailing app algorithms rather than core task automation.

occupations.title: Painting Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Painting trades require physical presence, manual dexterity, and spatial awareness to apply finishes to buildings and structures. AI and current digital technologies cannot perform these physical tasks. While AI might assist with minor administrative duties like quoting or scheduling, the core work remains entirely insulated from digital automation.

occupations.title: Keyboard Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Keyboard operators primarily perform routine data entry and document generation tasks, which are entirely computer-based. Advanced AI capabilities in optical character recognition, natural language processing, and automated data extraction can directly automate these core functions. Consequently, this occupation faces maximum exposure to AI disruption in the Australian market.

occupations.title: Retail Supervisors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Retail supervisors require a physical presence on the shop floor to manage staff, handle customer escalations, and oversee daily operations. While AI will streamline inventory management, rostering, and sales forecasting, the core interpersonal and physical aspects of the role provide a strong barrier to automation.

occupations.title: Fast Food Cooks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Fast Food Cooks perform highly physical, manual tasks in commercial kitchens, shielding them from current digital AI capabilities. While future robotics may eventually automate food preparation, generative AI and software cannot directly perform these physical duties. Any AI impact is limited to backend systems like inventory forecasting, leaving the core cooking tasks unaffected.

occupations.title: Computer Network Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Computer Network Professionals perform highly digital tasks such as network configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting, which are increasingly being automated by AI-driven network operations (AIOps) and infrastructure-as-code tools. While there is some physical hardware management involved, the bulk of the analytical and configuration work is done via computer, leading to very high AI exposure.

occupations.title: Social Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Social work is fundamentally reliant on deep interpersonal skills, empathy, and navigating complex, unpredictable human situations. While AI can significantly streamline case management, reporting, and administrative tasks, the core therapeutic and advocacy work requires a human presence.

occupations.title: Physiotherapists

While AI will increasingly streamline back-office tasks such as inventory forecasting, staff rostering, and financial reporting, the core responsibilities of hospitality management remain heavily reliant on human leadership and physical oversight.

occupations.title: Precision Metal Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Precision Metal Trades Workers perform highly manual, specialised physical tasks fabricating and repairing metal instruments. While AI may eventually assist in CAD/CAM design or diagnostics, the core work requires physical dexterity and presence, making it highly insulated from digital AI automation.

occupations.title: Chiropractors and Osteopaths
occupations.exposure_rationale: The core duties of chiropractors and osteopaths involve hands-on physical manipulation and tactile assessment of patients, which AI cannot replicate. While AI will streamline practice management, appointment scheduling, and clinical note-taking, the fundamental treatment requires in-person physical presence and human interaction.

occupations.title: Gallery, Library and Museum Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly streamline cataloguing, metadata generation, and library database management, a large portion of this role involves the physical handling, preparation, and exhibition of delicate artworks and artefacts. This blend of digital administration and specialised physical tasks results in a moderate level of AI exposure.

occupations.title: Complementary Health Therapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Complementary Health Therapists rely heavily on in-person consultations, physical therapies, and deep interpersonal connection to treat patients holistically. While AI can assist with practice management, appointment scheduling, and basic symptom checking, the core therapeutic relationship and physical aspects of the role are highly resistant to automation.

occupations.title: Meat Boners and Slicers, and Slaughterers
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is entirely physical and manual, taking place in abattoirs and meat processing facilities. Current digital AI capabilities cannot perform the physical tasks of slaughtering or butchering meat. While industrial automation and robotics may impact the sector eventually, exposure to digital AI over the next 5 years is minimal.

occupations.title: Metal Engineering Process Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Metal Engineering Process Workers perform physical, routine tasks in manufacturing environments. Digital AI has minimal direct application to these manual processes, as the work relies on physical presence and manual handling rather than data processing or knowledge work. While traditional automation or robotics may impact this field, current generative AI capabilities will not significantly reshape the day-to-day tasks.

occupations.title: Anaesthetists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Anaesthetists require physical presence in operating theatres, performing manual procedures like intubation and administering localized injections. While AI will increasingly assist in real-time patient monitoring and dosage optimization, the high-stakes, unpredictable nature of surgical environments and the need for direct physical intervention provide a strong barrier to automation.

occupations.title: Surgeons
occupations.exposure_rationale: Surgeons rely on highly precise manual skills and physical presence in operating theatres, making their core tasks highly resistant to current digital AI. While AI will increasingly assist in pre-operative planning, medical imaging analysis, and administrative duties, the actual physical intervention cannot be automated by software.

occupations.title: Visual Merchandisers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can assist in generating design concepts and planning layouts for retail displays, the core of a Visual Merchandiser's role involves the physical installation and arrangement of goods and props in stores. This hands-on, physical requirement in unpredictable retail environments provides a strong barrier to AI automation.

occupations.title: Chemical, Gas, Petroleum and Power Plant Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI and advanced control systems can optimize plant efficiency and predictive maintenance, these roles require on-site physical presence, safety-critical decision making, and manual intervention in industrial environments. The highly regulated and physical nature of the Australian energy and chemical sectors limits direct AI replacement, keeping exposure low.

occupations.title: Deck and Fishing Hands
occupations.exposure_rationale: Deck and fishing hands operate in highly unpredictable, physical marine environments where manual labour is essential. While AI may improve navigation or weather forecasting, the core tasks of maintaining ship structures and catching seafood cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Paving and Surfacing Labourers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Paving and surfacing labourers perform highly physical, outdoor work laying materials on roads and other surfaces. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate these manual tasks or navigate the unpredictable physical environments of construction sites, resulting in minimal AI exposure.

occupations.title: Authors, and Book and Script Editors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Authors and editors perform entirely computer-based work focused on text generation, evaluation, and refinement. Generative AI models are already highly capable of drafting, editing, and structuring written content, which will significantly reshape these roles. While human creativity and cultural context remain valuable, the core tasks are highly exposed to AI automation and augmentation.

occupations.title: Retail and Wool Buyers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Retail buying relies heavily on demand forecasting, trend analysis, and inventory management, tasks that AI can perform with high accuracy and efficiency. While wool buying involves some physical inspection of fleece quality, the broader market analysis and purchasing decisions are highly susceptible to digital automation. This will likely reduce the number of buyers needed, accelerating the already projected decline in employment.

occupations.title: Chemical and Materials Engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Chemical and Materials Engineers perform a significant amount of complex data analysis, system design, and specification drafting, which are highly exposed to AI optimization and materials discovery algorithms. However, their responsibilities also include supervising physical chemical plants and conducting real-world testing, which provides a barrier to full automation. In Australia's manufacturing and resources sectors, AI will act as a powerful tool to enhance productivity rather than completely replace these engineers.

occupations.title: Vehicle Body Builders and Trimmers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Vehicle body building and trimming is a highly manual trade requiring physical manipulation of materials, tools, and vehicle parts. While AI might assist in some minor design or administrative tasks, the core work of manufacturing, repairing, and installing physical components cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Debt Collectors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Debt collection relies heavily on routine communication, negotiation, and data processing, all of which are performed via computer or phone. AI-driven voice agents, chatbots, and predictive analytics are already highly capable of automating outreach and formalising payment arrangements, leading to very high AI exposure.

occupations.title: Library Assistants
occupations.exposure_rationale: While the physical task of shelving books provides a barrier to full automation, the core administrative duties of maintaining records and processing loans are highly susceptible to AI. In Australian libraries, self-service kiosks have already reduced demand, and AI will further streamline patron inquiries and inventory management.

occupations.title: Podiatrists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Podiatry is a highly physical and interpersonal healthcare profession requiring direct patient contact, manual treatments, and physical examinations. While AI will assist with administrative tasks, patient scheduling, and potentially diagnostic imaging analysis, the core clinical work cannot be automated.

occupations.title: Safety Inspectors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Safety Inspectors require physical presence to assess machines, equipment, and working conditions in varied environments like construction sites and factories. While AI can significantly streamline compliance reporting, data analysis, and regulatory research, the core requirement of on-site physical inspection limits overall exposure.

occupations.title: Other Clerical and Office Support Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: This category includes a mix of routine office tasks, such as classified advertising clerks, which are highly exposed to AI automation, and physical field roles like meter readers and parking inspectors. While the clerical components are highly vulnerable to digital AI, the physical presence required for the field roles provides a natural barrier, resulting in a moderate overall exposure.

occupations.title: Other Sales Support Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sales support roles heavily involve routine administrative tasks, order processing, and customer communication, which are highly exposed to AI automation through advanced CRMs and conversational AI. While some minor physical tasks may be required depending on the specific retail or wholesale environment, the core duties are predominantly digital and routine.

occupations.title: Sewing Machinists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sewing machinists perform highly physical and manual tasks operating industrial machinery and handling textiles. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate the physical manipulation of fabrics required in this role. While advanced robotics may eventually impact the industry, near-term digital AI exposure is minimal.

occupations.title: Electronics Engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Electronics engineering involves a substantial amount of computer-aided design, simulation, and embedded software coding, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role also requires physical prototyping, hardware testing, and installation, providing a natural barrier to full automation. AI will significantly boost productivity in the design phase, reshaping the role to focus more on complex systems integration and physical validation.

occupations.title: Signwriters
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI tools can heavily assist or even automate the design and layout phases of signwriting, the physical fabrication, painting, and installation on structures require manual trade skills. This blend of digital design and physical execution results in moderate AI exposure.

occupations.title: Caretakers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Caretaking involves physical maintenance, cleaning, and groundskeeping across various facilities, requiring manual dexterity and physical presence. occupations.title: Driving Instructors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Driving instruction requires physical presence in a vehicle, real-time observation, and interpersonal coaching to ensure safety and build learner confidence. occupations.title: Street Vendors and Related Salespersons
occupations.exposure_rationale: Street vending and door-to-door sales require physical presence, mobility, and face-to-face interpersonal skills to engage customers in real-world environments. While AI may assist with backend inventory or route planning, the core tasks of setting up stalls, handling physical goods, and direct human persuasion are highly insulated from digital automation.

occupations.title: Industrial Spraypainters
occupations.exposure_rationale: Industrial spraypainting is a highly physical and manual occupation requiring on-site presence to operate machinery and apply coatings to manufactured goods. While physical robotics have automated some painting tasks in manufacturing, current digital AI capabilities like language models and data analysis have virtually no direct application to the core duties of this role.

occupations.title: Other Hospitality Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: These roles involve physical tasks in hospitality environments, such as clearing tables or carrying luggage, which require physical presence and manual effort. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate these physical and interpersonal services. AI exposure is minimal, limited perhaps to minor scheduling or backend hotel management software.

occupations.title: Gallery, Museum and Tour Guides
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI-driven audio guides, translation apps, and interactive digital avatars can provide information to visitors, the core role of a guide involves physical presence, crowd management, and interpersonal engagement. In the Australian tourism and cultural sector, the human element and local storytelling remain highly valued, limiting the extent to which AI can fully replace these workers.

occupations.title: Florists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Floristry is a highly manual trade requiring the delicate physical handling and arrangement of flowers, which AI cannot replicate. While AI tools can assist Australian florists with small business administration, inventory forecasting, and social media marketing, the core value proposition remains entirely physical and interpersonal.

occupations.title: Railway Track Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Railway track work is highly physical and takes place in unpredictable outdoor environments, including remote mining and regional rail networks in Australia. occupations.title: Telemarketers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Telemarketing involves highly routine, script-based communication that can be entirely managed via digital and telecommunication systems. occupations.title: Mechanical Engineering Draftspersons, Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: Drafting and CAD tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven generative design and automated modelling tools, which will significantly streamline the design phase. occupations.title: Diversional Therapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Diversional therapists work heavily in Australia's aged care and NDIS sectors, requiring deep interpersonal skills, empathy, and physical presence to facilitate activities. occupations.title: Funeral Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Funeral workers perform highly physical tasks such as preparing bodies, combined with deeply empathetic, interpersonal duties when interacting with grieving families. occupations.title: Paper and Wood Processing Machine Operators

occupations.title: Economists

occupations.title: Wood Machinists and Other Wood Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: The core tasks of wood machinists and trades workers involve the physical manipulation of materials, operating machinery, and hands-on craftsmanship. occupations.title: Vending Machine Attendants

occupations.title: Commissioned Officers (Management)
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with logistics, intelligence analysis, and administrative planning, the core of this role involves high-stakes decision-making, human leadership, and accountability in unpredictable real-world environments like defence and emergency services. The necessity for interpersonal command and strategic judgment limits the overall AI exposure.

occupations.title: Primary Products Inspectors

occupations.title: Nurserypersons
occupations.exposure_rationale: Nursery work is highly physical, involving the hands-on propagation, cultivation, and care of plants in unpredictable biological environments. occupations.title: Personal Care Consultants

occupations.title: Food Trades Assistants
occupations.exposure_rationale: Food Trades Assistants perform highly physical, manual tasks such as cleaning, food preparation, and storage in commercial kitchens. occupations.title: Gaming Workers

occupations.title: Outdoor Adventure Guides

occupations.title: Sheetmetal Trades Workers

occupations.title: Jewellers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Jewellers perform highly skilled manual work involving physical materials, which digital AI cannot automate. While AI may assist in generating design concepts or managing small business administration, the core tasks of crafting, repairing, and polishing jewellery require fine motor skills and physical presence.

occupations.title: Timber and Wood Process Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Timber and Wood Process Workers perform highly physical, manual tasks in industrial environments like sawmills and timber yards. Digital AI capabilities such as language processing and data analysis have virtually no direct application to these physical duties. While traditional industrial automation and robotics may impact the sector, exposure to digital AI is minimal.

occupations.title: Senior Non-Commissioned Defence Force Officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Senior NCOs in the Defence Force primarily focus on leadership, personnel management, and enforcing directives in physical and often unpredictable environments. occupations.title: Teachers of English to Speakers of Other Languages
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI language models and advanced tutoring applications are highly capable of automating lesson planning, grading, and providing conversational practice. occupations.title: Telecommunications Technical Specialists
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly automate network monitoring, predictive maintenance, and technical support diagnostics, the physical installation and hardware troubleshooting of telecommunications equipment require manual skills and on-site presence. This blend of digital network management and physical infrastructure work results in moderate AI exposure.

occupations.title: Print Finishers and Screen Printers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Print finishing and screen printing are highly physical trades involving the operation of machinery and handling of physical materials. occupations.title: ICT Trainers

occupations.title: Special Care Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Special Care Workers operate in highly unpredictable, interpersonal environments providing direct physical and emotional support to vulnerable individuals. occupations.title: Recycling and Rubbish Collectors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Recycling and rubbish collection is an inherently physical occupation requiring presence in unpredictable outdoor environments across Australian municipalities. occupations.title: Caravan Park and Camping Ground Managers

occupations.title: Mixed Crop and Livestock Farm Workers

occupations.title: Boat Builders and Shipwrights
occupations.exposure_rationale: Boat building and shipwright work is highly physical and manual, requiring specialized craftsmanship in real-world environments like shipyards and marinas. While AI might assist in initial design or administrative tasks, the core construction, fitting, and repair work cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Auctioneers, and Stock and Station Agents
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with market analysis, drafting property listings, and administrative tasks, the core role relies heavily on interpersonal relationships, physical inspection of livestock and rural properties, and live auctioneering. In the Australian agricultural context, trust and local knowledge are paramount, providing a strong barrier to significant AI displacement.

occupations.title: Metal Casting, Forging and Finishing Trades
occupations.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves highly physical and manual tasks such as heating, hammering, and polishing metal. AI cannot perform these physical fabrication and finishing tasks. While AI might assist in minor administrative or design processes, the core work remains entirely reliant on human manual skill and physical presence.

occupations.title: Agricultural Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: Agricultural technicians in Australia split their time between physical field or lab work, such as collecting soil samples and running crop trials, and data analysis. While AI will significantly streamline data processing, predictive modelling, and report generation, the core physical tasks of gathering real-world agricultural samples and setting up experiments remain highly insulated from digital AI.

occupations.title: Legislators
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role of a legislator is fundamentally representative and legally requires an elected human official, meaning AI cannot reduce headcount regardless of productivity gains. While AI will heavily assist with drafting policy, analyzing constituent sentiment, and writing speeches, the core duties of political negotiation, public representation, and decision-making are highly interpersonal and insulated from automation.

occupations.title: Shearers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Shearing is an intensely physical and manual job requiring the handling of live, unpredictable animals in rural Australian settings. occupations.title: Clay, Concrete, Glass and Stone Processing Machine Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role involves operating physical machinery to process raw materials like clay, concrete, and stone in manufacturing environments. Digital AI has minimal direct application to these manual, hands-on tasks. While industrial automation and robotics may impact the sector, software-based AI will not significantly reshape the day-to-day work.

occupations.title: Switchboard Operators

occupations.title: Printing Assistants and Table Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Printing Assistants and Table Workers perform highly manual and physical tasks such as operating bindery machines and finishing printed products. occupations.title: Forestry and Logging Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Forestry and logging work is intensely physical and takes place in highly unpredictable, unstructured outdoor environments across Australia's regional areas. While AI may optimize supply chains or forestry management at a corporate level, the day-to-day physical role of these labourers will remain largely unaffected over the next five years.

occupations.title: Plastics and Rubber Factory Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Plastics and rubber factory work is inherently physical and manual, requiring workers to operate machinery and handle materials on a production floor. occupations.title: Aquaculture Farmers

occupations.title: Upholsterers

occupations.title: Toolmakers and Engineering Patternmakers

occupations.title: Textile and Footwear Production Machine Operators

occupations.title: Graphic Pre-press Trades Workers

occupations.title: Photographic Developers and Printers
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI-driven image processing and editing tools can automate the vast majority of digital adjustments, color corrections, and retouching tasks. occupations.title: Indigenous Health Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Indigenous Health Workers rely heavily on deep cultural knowledge, interpersonal trust, and in-person community engagement to deliver health care. occupations.title: Canvas and Leather Goods Makers

occupations.title: Middle School Teachers

occupations.title: Aquaculture Workers
